name: AI Foundation Model Provider (EU market)
description: Curated framework bundle for a provider of a general-purpose AI model (GPAI) — including those with systemic risk under EU AI Act Art. 51 — placing the model on the EU market or making it available to deployers in the EU.

crosswalk_pairs.rationale: Art. 9 / 11 / 12 / 17 risk-management, technical documentation, record-keeping and QMS obligations map directly to ISO 42001 Cl. 6.1 / 7.2 / 9.1 / 4.1 — 42001 is the standards-based way to evidence the GPAI / high-risk AI QMS.
Art. 9 maps to NIST AI RMF MAP-1 / MEASURE-1 / MANAGE-1; Art. 14 to GOVERN-2; Art. 15 to MEASURE-2 — NIST AI RMF gives operational substance to the Act's process requirements.
Training-data ingestion and inference logging both fall under Art. 32 — re-use ISO 27001 Annex A.5 / A.8 evidence for the security narrative.

sequencing_notes: 1. EU AI Act Art. 53 (GPAI obligations) is the gating release prerequisite — technical documentation per Annex XI, training-data summary per Art. 53(1)(d), copyright-policy disclosure per Art. 53(1)(c), downstream-provider information per Art. 53(1)(b) MUST be ready before customer-facing release in the EU.

2. If the model crosses the systemic-risk threshold (Art. 51 — currently 10^25 FLOPs cumulative compute), Art. 55 obligations add model-evaluation, adversarial-testing, serious-incident reporting and cybersecurity protection — plan for these from training-run inception, not post-hoc.
3. ISO 42001 AIMS in parallel — 42001 Cl. 4.1 / 6.1 / 7.5 / 9.1 give the auditable QMS shape the AI Act assumes; certification is the most defensible posture against EU-AI-Office inquiries.

4. GDPR for training data — lawful basis (Art. 6 / 9), DPIA (Art. 35) for large-scale scraping, transparency (Art. 13 / 14) to data subjects whose data is in training corpora, plus data-subject rights (Art. 15–22) including the right against solely-automated decisions.

5. NIST AI RMF as the operational profile — its MAP / MEASURE / MANAGE / GOVERN structure documents day-to-day risk-handling that satisfies both EU AI Act Art. 9 and many US enterprise-AI questionnaires.
6. ISO 27001 + NIS2 if you operate the inference infrastructure yourself — the model-API serving plane is in scope as an 'important entity' digital service under NIS2 Annex II.
